mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS above prior 12-month average signals an active tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is continuously active and does not require a discrete trigger.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.57% in Feb 2023, near cycle high
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following rate increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points above its trailing 3-month cyclical low, it marks an inflection from a tight labor market to labor-market deterioration, which historically precedes earnings downgrades and a negative S&P 500 return over the following quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp above its trailing 3-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% in Feb 2023, near historic lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market at cyclical tightness
failure_condition: In >55% of historical trigger instances since 2005, forward 60d SPX returns are positive despite the UNRATE uptick trigger being active, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated (above trend) while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously at or near a cycle high, the implied real policy rate is restrictive and rising, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this; the joint state of high nominal rates against still-high inflation determines the restrictiveness of policy.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 301.45 in Feb 2023 with FEDFUNDS at 4.57%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns during joint high-CPI/high-FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly more negative than periods with either condition alone (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, a decline in HOUST of more than 5% over a 3-month window signals housing-sector demand contraction, which propagates to broader consumer and construction-linked earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. Outside tightening regimes, housing softness has weaker equity transmission because financial conditions are not simultaneously restrictive.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised down from 1361.0 to 1309.0 vintage Jan 2023 amid FEDFUNDS at 4.57%
failure_condition: Housing starts declines under active tightening are followed by positive or flat 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of historical trigger instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and near-term corporate revenue strength, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incorporate incrementally favorable production data. This is a continuously active momentum effect requiring no discrete trigger.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 102.9525 in Jan 2023, later revised to 100.505
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly momentum and forward 5d SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or effect reverses sign persistently across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month average of PAYEMS net job gains falls below 150,000, it signals a material deceleration in labor demand that foreshadows slower consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS net change falls below 150,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 155,350k Feb 2023 revised down to 155,066k
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following sub-150k PAYEMS 3-month average readings are positive in more than 55% of historical instances since 2005, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Resilient nominal retail sales growth (RSAFS) sustained despite an elevated FEDFUNDS level indicates the consumer sector is absorbing tighter financial conditions without material demand destruction, supporting near-term equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect requires the joint condition of strong RSAFS growth AND high FEDFUNDS; RSAFS strength alone under low rates carries no special signal.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer
failure_condition: Forward 5d SPX returns following the joint RSAFS-strength/high-FEDFUNDS trigger are not significantly more positive than unconditional baseline returns (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime combining a cycle-high FEDFUNDS rate with persistently elevated CPIAUCSL, acute financial-sector stress events (e.g., bank liquidity strains under rapid rate repricing) are more likely to propagate into broad equity selloffs because the central bank has limited room to ease, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter until policy stance visibly shifts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS at or near multi-year high while CPIAUCSL YoY > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 4.57% and CPIAUCSL 301.45 as of Feb 2023 amid banking sector stress in March 2023
regime_hint: banking-sector stress under restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns during periods meeting the high-FEDFUNDS/high-CPI regime trigger are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.
